description: Fix the deterministic heuristic planner (lib/plan-builder.ts) so a narrowly-scoped request (e.g. 'build me a schema in AEP') produces a plan containing ONLY the modules genuinely implied by the request, instead of cascading into Launch/CJA/AJO via synthetic defaults or 'empty means yes' fallbacks. Depends on FEAT-001 (baseline established). This is the reproducible, testable core of the user's complaint: 'if I need to build a schema in AEP, the agents should NOT be calling CJA or AJO or even Launch/data collection.'
steps: In lib/plan-builder.ts, add an explicit 'has this config got REAL (non-default/non-synthetic) signal for X' set of checks, distinguishing user-specified events/segments from synthetic defaults that aepModule/launchModule currently inject (the 'engaged_visitors' default segment in aepModule.build() and the 'page_view' default event in launchModule.build()). The core bug: downstream modules (cjaModule, ajoActivationModule, launchModule) key their isApplicable() off config.segments.length>0 / config.events.length>0 AFTER those defaults may already exist in the SAME config object reference passed through planUseCase() -- confirm whether isApplicable() checks run against the ORIGINAL config (pre-injection) or a mutated one; currently each module's build() only mutates its own module-local `steps`/`ctx`, not `config.segments`/`config.events` themselves, so config.segments/config.events reaching cjaModule/launchModule/ajoActivationModule's isApplicable() should already reflect only what the user/enrichment actually supplied -- verify this via the code and add regression coverage for it (see FEAT-002 verification script) since the reported bug may instead stem from intent-extractor.ts synthesizing false positives from broad regex keywords (e.g. PLANNER_KEYWORDS.md's own event table maps very broad phrases to events) or from the MCP's own upstream planner_parse_natural_language already returning a non-empty default segment/event for ANY request (documented in MCP_TOOLS_REFERENCE.md's SolutionConfig shape) -- so the fix must be robust regardless of upstream source: introduce an explicit way to know 'this config's segments/events are real user-requested items, not planner filler' OR simply stop launchModule/cjaModule/ajoActivationModule from treating presence-of-any-segment/event as sufficient signal on its own.

Change ajoActivationModule.isApplicable() in lib/plan-builder.ts: remove the `destinations.length === 0` branch of `wantsActivation` (currently: `const wantsActivation = destinations.length === 0 || destinations.some(...)`). An empty destinations array must NOT imply activation is wanted. Only fire when destinations explicitly include an activation channel (email/web/push/sms), AND update ajoActivationModule.reason() to no longer claim 'No destinations specified, so activation is assumed by default.'

Change cjaModule.isApplicable() in lib/plan-builder.ts: it currently fires whenever `hasSegments` (ANY segments exist) OR `profile.analyticsFocused`. Tighten so mere segment presence is not sufficient on its own -- CJA should only be included when the use case has an explicit analytics/reporting signal (profile.analyticsFocused, which itself is derived from classifyUseCase()'s analyticsSignals/destinations.includes('crm')) or when the request explicitly asks for reporting/analytics on top of segments. Update cjaModule.reason() to match the new logic.

Change aepModule.build() in lib/plan-builder.ts: when config.segments is empty, it currently still injects a synthetic 'engaged_visitors' default segment step. Change this so aepModule does NOT create a default segment when the user asked for zero segments -- only create segment steps for segments actually present in config.segments (skip segment creation entirely if the array is empty). This removes the root cause of downstream modules seeing 'a segment exists' when really none was ever asked for.

Change launchModule.build() in lib/plan-builder.ts similarly: when config.events is empty, it currently injects a synthetic default 'page_view' event and builds a full Launch property/rule/library sequence around it. Since launchModule.isApplicable() already requires `config.events.length > 0` to be applicable at all, this default-event injection inside build() is dead weight IF isApplicable is honored correctly -- but double check by tracing planUseCase(): if isApplicable() is false, build() is never called, so remove the internal eventList fallback-to-default entirely (it should never trigger); keep a defensive fallback only as a safety net with a comment explaining it should be unreachable, or remove it if it truly is unreachable and simplifies the code.

Update classifyUseCase(config) in lib/plan-builder.ts if needed so that a schema-only config (segments=[], events=[], destinations=[], goals=[], personalization_placements=[]) classifies as general-purpose (no activation/analytics/personalization focus) and needsDataCollection=false, and confirm this doesn't change behavior for legitimate activation/analytics/personalization configs.

Review lib/intent-extractor.ts's EVENT_KEYWORDS/SEGMENT_KEYWORDS/DESTINATION_KEYWORDS/PLACEMENT_KEYWORDS/GOAL_KEYWORDS tables for any keyword broad enough to false-positive on a narrow schema-only request like 'build me a schema in AEP' (e.g. does 'schema' or 'AEP' accidentally match any keyword regex today? Trace through matchAll() with that exact string). If a false-positive match is found, tighten the specific regex; do not do a wholesale rewrite of the keyword tables.

Add a lightweight verification script (e.g. scripts/verify-planning.ts, run via `npx tsx scripts/verify-planning.ts`) that imports planUseCase from lib/plan-builder.ts and classifyUseCase, and runs it against at least 3 representative SolutionConfig fixtures: (a) schema-only (website_domain set, business_vertical set, everything else empty arrays/strings) -> assert module 'included' list is exactly ['rag','aep'] (or whatever the correct minimal set is per the fix) and specifically assert 'launch','cja','ajo_activation','ajo_offers' are NOT included; (b) full activation ask (events + segments + destinations=['email']) -> assert launch+cja+ajo_activation are included; (c) analytics-only ask (segments present, destinations=[], goals=['improve_reporting']) -> assert cja is included but ajo_activation and launch are NOT (unless events are present). Print each ModulePlanSummary list to stdout for manual inspection too.
Run `npm run build` to confirm the change type-checks, and run the verification script to confirm the three fixtures behave as expected.
acceptance_criteria: For a schema-only SolutionConfig (no events/segments/destinations/placements/goals beyond website_domain+business_vertical), planUseCase() returns a ModulePlanSummary list where only 'rag' and 'aep' have included=true; 'launch', 'cja', 'ajo_activation', and 'ajo_offers' all have included=false.
aepModule no longer creates a segment step when config.segments is empty (verify aep step count for the schema-only fixture contains schema+dataset steps only, no aep_segment_* step).
ajoActivationModule.isApplicable() returns false for a config with segments present but destinations=[] (previously this returned true via the 'destinations.length === 0' default-activation branch).
For a full activation ask (events+segments+destinations=['email']), launch, cja, and ajo_activation are all still included=true (no regression for legitimate multi-module use cases).
`npm run build` passes with exit code 0 after the change.
verification: Run `npx tsx scripts/verify-planning.ts` (or equivalent script created in this feature) and confirm printed module-inclusion lists match the acceptance criteria for all three fixtures.
Run `npm run build` and confirm exit code 0.
Run `npm run lint` and confirm no new lint errors were introduced (pre-existing warnings from FEAT-001 baseline are acceptable).
findings: Root cause confirmed and fixed in lib/plan-builder.ts (no changes needed in lib/intent-extractor.ts - traced EVENT_KEYWORDS/SEGMENT_KEYWORDS/PLACEMENT_KEYWORDS/GOAL_KEYWORDS/DESTINATION_KEYWORDS against the literal string 'build me a schema in AEP' with a standalone node script and confirmed zero false-positive matches, so the cascade bug lived entirely in plan-builder.ts's gating logic, not the regex tables). Five changes made: (1) aepModule.build() no longer injects a synthetic 'engaged_visitors' default segment when config.segments is empty - only real segments produce aep_segment_* steps. (2) ajoActivationModule.isApplicable() no longer treats an empty destinations array as 'activation assumed by default' - removed the `destinations.length === 0` branch from wantsActivation, now requires an explicit activation-channel destination (email/web/push/sms); updated .reason() to match. (3) classifyUseCase()'s analyticsSignals no longer treats `destinations.length === 0` as an analytics signal (this was the deepest root cause - it made ANY request with no destinations classify as analytics-focused, which fed cjaModule.isApplicable()'s `profile.analyticsFocused` branch); now analyticsSignals requires an explicit reporting/engagement/conversion goal. cjaModule.isApplicable() itself was left as `hasSegments || profile.analyticsFocused` per FEAT-002.json's own guidance ('even after fixing #1, consider whether mere segment presence should be sufficient') - once aepModule stopped synthesizing default segments, hasSegments now only reflects genuinely user-requested segments, so it remains a valid standalone signal for CJA reporting (verified via fixture B: a real segment + email destination + reporting goal correctly includes cja; fixture C: real segment alone with a reporting goal also correctly includes cja). (4) launchModule.build()'s internal 'page_view' default-event fallback was removed entirely (not just documented) since isApplicable() already requires config.events.length>0 and build() is only ever called when isApplicable() is true, making the fallback genuinely dead code; replaced with an explanatory comment instead of a defensive default, since a silent fallback would mask rather than surface any future isApplicable regression. Same dead-code cleanup applied to ajoActivationModule.build()'s segmentList fallback and cjaModule.build()'s primarySegmentName fallback (both used 'engaged_visitors' fallbacks that could never trigger post-fix). Verification: created scripts/verify-planning.ts (npx tsx scripts/verify-planning.ts) with 3 fixtures - (A) schema-only: only 'rag'+'aep' included, launch/cja/ajo_activation/ajo_offers excluded, and aep produces zero aep_segment_* steps - PASS. (B) full activation (events+segments+email destination+reporting goal): launch+cja+ajo_activation all included - PASS. (C) analytics-only (segments+reporting goal, no destinations/events): cja included, ajo_activation+launch excluded - PASS. All 3 fixtures pass with exit code 0. `npm run build` passes with exit code 0. `npm run lint` shows exactly 54 errors (0 warnings) - identical count/composition to the FEAT-001 baseline, confirming no new lint errors were introduced by this change.
